unit: Department of Physiology, Life Sciences Complex

description: Advanced multiscale imaging (STED, light‑sheet, nano‑CT), automation/robotics, and quantitative data analysis infrastructure

unit: Victor Phillip Dahdaleh Institute-McGill Genome Centre
description: High-throughput sequencing (Illumina, Nanopore) with automated workflows.
access: Per-sample or per-run sequencing/genotyping costs; internal vs external pricing tiers

unit: Victor Phillip Dahdaleh Institute-McGill Genome Centre
description: Provides behavioral phenotyping services in animal models, including cognitive, emotional, and motor assessments, with experimental design support and data analysis.
access: Per-session or per-experiment animal testing fees; technician support billed separately

description: Offers bioinformatics support for genomics and multi-omics data analysis, including pipelines, statistics, and interpretation.
access: Hourly consulting + project-based billing

description: Custom bioinformatics pipelines, software development and large-scale data analysis.
access: Project-based pricing; grants/subsidized academic rates; full-cost recovery for external users

description: TEM and SEM imaging with sample prep and expert assistance.
access: Hourly instrument time + training fees; assisted service billed separately

description: Multi-laser flow cytometers, cell sorting and training.
access: Per-hour instrument use + per-sample fees; assisted sorting higher cost

description: Targeted and untargeted metabolomics via LC-MS and GC-MS.
access: Per-sample analysis pricing; package deals for large studies

description: Tissue processing, embedding, sectioning and staining services.
access: Per-sample/service (embedding, sectioning, staining); à la carte pricing

platform: Single-Cell Imaging & Mass Cytometry Analysis Platform (SCIMAP)
description: Mass cytometry, single-cell imaging and data analysis.
access: Per-run or per-sample for mass cytometry; high-cost, service-heavy model

platform: Centre for Research in Biophysical & Structural Biology (CRBS)
description: Integrated structural biology platform providing access to X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM sample preparation, NMR spectroscopy, biophysical characterization, and computational structural analysis.
access: Mix of membership-style access + per-use fees depending on equipment

platform:
Imaging and Molecular
Biology Platform
 (Pharmacology & Therapeutics)
description: Provides advanced imaging and molecular biology services, including microscopy, assays, and molecular techniques.
access: Per-use instrument fees + reagent/service charges

description: Provides biomolecular interaction analysis (SPR) and protein characterization via mass spectrometry.
access: Per-experiment or hourly instrument use; highly specialized service pricing

description: Supports research on infection and inflammatory diseases, including experimental models and biomarker analysis.
access: Service-based fees (assays, models); often project-based quotes

platform:
McGill Integrated Core
for Animal Modeling
description: Provides generation, maintenance, and phenotyping of genetically modified animal models, including CRISPR engineering.
access: Per-animal/per-procedure costs; housing + technical services billed separately

domain: Functional Genomics / Cell Biology / CRISPR Screening
description: Provides tools and expertise for cellular perturbation, including CRISPR-based screening and functional genomics.
access: Per-screen or per-library usage; project-based pricing

unit: Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering / Department of Physics
platform: McGill Nanotools Micro- and Nanofabrication Facility
description: Cleanroom-based micro- and nanofabrication facility offering lithography, thin-film deposition, etching, and device fabrication, with staff training and technical support.
access: Cleanroom access hourly rates + training fees + material costs

description: High-throughput sequencing, library preparation, and genomics workflows integrated within IRIC research infrastructure.
access: Per-sample / per-run pricing (sequencing, library prep); tiered internal vs external rates

description: Bioinformatics pipelines, data analysis, and computational support for genomics and functional studies.
access: Hourly consulting + project-based pricing; embedded collaboration models

description: Mass spectrometry-based proteomics and protein characterization services.
access: Per-sample analysis (mass spec) + optional data analysis fees

description: Small-molecule libraries, compound screening, and chemical biology expertise.
access: Project-based pricing (chemical biology services, compound development)

description: Automated high-throughput screening using cellular and biochemical assays.
access: Per-screen or per-compound library access; project-based cost

description: Multi-laser flow cytometry and cell sorting with technical support.
access: Hourly instrument usage + per-sample sorting fees; assisted services extra

description: Investigation of protein structure, protein-ligand interactions and routine small molecule analysis.
access: Hourly instrument time (NMR, biophysics tools) + staff support fees

description: Provides scientific and technical expertise, veterinary support, and housing of rodents for research using murine models, including transgenesis, animal modeling, pharmacokinetics, and toxicity studies. (iric.ca)
access: Per-animal / per-procedure costs; includes housing and technical services

description: Provides access to advanced microscopy systems for 2D, 3D, and high-resolution imaging, along with training, consultation, and image analysis workstations. (iric.ca)
access: Hourly microscope usage + training + assisted imaging services

description: Provides tissue processing and analysis services including embedding, sectioning, staining, immunohistochemistry, and digital slide imaging for research applications. (iric.ca)
access: Per-sample / per-service (embedding, staining, sectioning)

domain: Drug Discovery / Medicinal Chemistry / Chemical Biology
description: Designs and synthesizes biologically active small molecules, supports hit-to-lead optimization, SAR studies, computational chemistry, and preclinical development in collaborative research projects. (iric.ca)
access: Fully project-based pricing

unit: Faculté des arts et des sciences
platform: Plateforme de Microscopie - Institut Courtois d'innovation biomédicale
description: Confocal and widefield microscopy with user training and support.
access: Hourly instrument access + training fees; internal vs external rates

unit: Faculté des arts et des sciences
description: Flow cytometry services for basic and translational research.
access: Per-hour instrument use + per-sample sorting; assisted service premium

unit: Faculté des arts et des sciences
description: Transmission and scanning electron microscopy with expert support.
access: Hourly instrument time + sample prep costs; training required

description: Advanced imaging platform dedicated to the study of visual neuroscience, comprising a wide range of state-of-the-art neurophotonics technologies, including confocal, multiphoton, light-sheet microscopy, in vivo imaging systems, and optogenetic stimulation tools. Also includes high-performance computing for image analysis and large data processing. The platform supports fundamental and translational research, enabling high-resolution imaging of neural and visual systems. (opto.umontreal.ca)
access: Hourly usage + project-based imaging services depending on complexity

description: Offers sequencing and resequencing services for genomes, transcriptomes and amplicons including sample preparation, library preparation and sequencing using platforms such as Illumina NextSeq and MiSeq. Also supports single-cell genomics (10X Genomics) and genotyping services.
access: Fee-for-service model with lower preferential rates for CERMO-FC members. Differential pricing for academic and industrial users, typically charged per sample, with additional costs for specialized primers, high-volume discounts, and optional administrative fees.

description: Provides analysis of next-generation sequencing datasets including DNA-seq, RNA-seq, methylation sequencing, ATAC-seq and single-cell datasets. Offers functional analysis, visualization, and training in computational tools.
access: Preferential pricing is available for CERMO-FC members, UQ network researchers, and academic researchers upon request, with bioinformatics services billed at a rate of $120/hour.

platform: Génération et phénotypage de modèles murins
description: Provides generation of genetically modified mouse models using transgenesis and CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing. Includes microinjection, embryo transfer, cryopreservation and in vitro fertilization for line recovery.
access: Preferential academic pricing for CERMO-FC and UQ network researchers, with transgenic mouse production costing $3,000 for academics vs $6,000 for industry, and CRISPR microinjection services priced at $3,000 per session for academics vs $6,000 for industry, with additional fees for DNA constructs and a possible 15% administrative surcharge depending on project specifics.

description: Offers integrated services in photonic imaging and flow cytometry including confocal microscopy, live-cell imaging, histological sectioning and cell sorting using instruments such as BD FACSMelody.
access: Preferential academic rates for CERMO-FC and UQ researchers, with usage priced at $35/hour for autonomous use or $75/hour with technical assistance, compared to $100/hour for industry users (including assistance), with a possible 15% administrative fee applied.

description: Provides analysis of biological and chemical molecules using mass spectrometry systems coupled with liquid chromatography (HPLC-MS-TOF, HPLC-MS/MS).
access: Preferential academic pricing for CERMO-FC and UQ researchers, with sample preparation and usage fees lower for academics (15$/sample prep and 60$/sample or 150$/hour) than industry (25$/sample prep and 150$/sample or 400$/hour), with possible volume discounts for >50 samples and an additional ~15% administrative fee when applicable.

description: Provides screening and characterization of biomolecular interactions using technologies such as SPR (Biacore), circular dichroism spectroscopy, plate-based assays, and peptide synthesis.
access: Preferential academic pricing for CERMO-FC and UQ researchers, charging $30/hour for instrument use and $30/hour for technical assistance (excluding microchip costs), compared to $140/hour for use and $65/hour for assistance for industrial users, with an additional potential 15% administrative fee depending on the project.

platform: Centre for Microscopy and Cellular Imaging (CMCI)
description: Confocal, fluorescence, and widefield microscopy with technical support and user training for cellular and tissue imaging.
access: Fee-for-service model with microscope usage typically ranging from $15–25/hour for Concordia researchers, plus training ($50/hour) and technical support ($60/hour); external academic users pay approximately 2× standard rates and industry users 4× standard rates

platform: Centre for Biological Applications of Mass Spectrometry (CBAMS)
description: Advanced LC-MS and MS/MS instrumentation for small molecules, metabolites, peptides, and proteins, with method development and data interpretation support.
access: Fee-for-service model with preferential rates for Concordia researchers, external academic users typically paying around $100/hour for instrument access, and industry users $250/hour, plus per-sample fees for analytical services and consultation/training charges for non-Concordia users.

platform: Centre for Structural and Functional Genomics (CSFG)
description: Functional genomics expertise including gene expression analysis, molecular assays, and data interpretation.
access: Fee-for-service model with service-specific pricing available upon request and charges based on instrument use, analytical support, and equipment maintenance costs.

description: Interdisciplinary research centre focused on aging across the life course, including health, wellbeing, policy, and community engagement, working closely with older adults and stakeholders. (Concordia University)

platform: Centre for Research in Molecular Modeling
description: Provides computational tools and expertise for molecular modeling, structural biology, and simulation of biomolecular systems.

platform: Centre for the Arts in Human Development (CAHD)
domain: Arts in Health / Therapy / Human Development
description: Uses arts-based approaches (music, visual arts, drama) to support therapeutic, educational, and developmental outcomes in clinical and community populations.

platform: Centre for the Study of Learning and Performance (CSLP)
description: Conducts research on learning, teaching, and performance across educational contexts, including literacy, cognition, and knowledge transfer.

platform: Center for Studies in Behavioral Neurobiology (CSBN)
description: Research centre studying brain mechanisms underlying behavior and disease, including addiction, cognition, circadian rhythms, and neurophysiology, using multidisciplinary approaches. (Concordia University)
Interdisciplinary research on sensory perception, culture, and society, exploring how the senses shape human experience and knowledge.

platform: Centre for Clinical Research in Health (CCRH)
description: Supports clinical and health research, including patient-based studies, behavioral health, and translational research in human populations. (Concordia University)
contact: Patti Ranahan, Jean-Philippe Gouin and Joyce H. L. Lui

description: Provides injury assessment, rehabilitation services, and research support in athletic therapy and musculoskeletal health.
access: Fee-for-service model with access is free of charge for approved Concordia School of Health researchers and trainees through the BookR reservation system.

description: Supports research on cardiopulmonary function, including exercise testing, respiratory analysis, and cardiovascular health assessment.
access: Fee-for-service model with instrument-specific hourly rates and lower discounted or free rates for Concordia internal users.

description: Provides clinical sample collection and analysis, supporting biomarker research and human health studies.
access: Instrument access and analytical support with technician assistance or student training available on request, with hourly usage fees of $60/hour for Concordia internal groups, $75/hour for external academic or public groups, and $90/hour for industry/private users.

description: Provides exercise equipment and space for intervention studies, training protocols, and community-based health research. (Concordia University)
access: Fee-for-service model with instrument-specific hourly rates and lower discounted or free rates for Concordia internal users.

description: Supports assessment of movement, strength, balance, and gait using motion capture, EMG, and force measurement systems. (Concordia University)
Provides imaging technologies for studying human structure and function, supporting interdisciplinary health research.
access: Hourly imaging services with rates ranging from approximately $50–$275/hour for Concordia internal users, $90–$650/hour for external academic/non-profit users, and $175–$1,400/hour for industry users depending on modality

description: Supports dietary assessment, nutritional interventions, and metabolic research in clinical and population health studies.
Provides tools for studying physiological responses to exercise and interventions, including metabolic and functional testing.
access: Fee-for-service model with instrument-specific hourly rates and lower discounted or free rates for Concordia internal users.

description: Supports research on sleep physiology, disorders, and circadian rhythms using monitoring and diagnostic tools.
access: Fee-for-service model with access is free of charge for approved Concordia School of Health researchers and trainees through the BookR reservation system.

description: Secure servers, research data storage, and computing infrastructure for data-intensive research.
access: Free access for Gina Cody School faculty, staff, and students (with supervisor/instructor approval for students), while non-GCS users require faculty sponsorship or Dean’s approval, and other Concordia users must request access through the Advanced Research Computing team.

description: Plateforme dédiée à l’imagerie médicale de pointe, équipée de technologies avancées telles que l’IRM, le CT-scan et le C-arm. Elle est destinée exclusivement à la recherche et à la formation, permettant la réalisation d’études cliniques, d’essais scientifiques et de formations avancées en imagerie médicale. (Université du Québec à Trois-Rivières)
access: The service provides access to institutional research and administrative support tools and resources for UQTR members, including digital platforms for academic, teaching, and research activities, with access generally restricted to active UQTR users.

platform: Animalerie de formation et de recherche
description: Infrastructure providing housing and experimental support for rodent models (mice and rats), compliant with Canadian Council on Animal Care standards. (Université du Québec à Trois-Rivières)
access: Shared institutional research infrastructure governed under formal platform structures, operating on a user-pay model with an established fee schedule

platform: Centre d'apprentissage et de simulation en sciences infirmières (CASSI)
description: Simulation-based learning environment replicating real clinical settings to develop clinical judgment and practical skills in nursing. (Université du Québec à Trois-Rivières)
access: Shared institutional research infrastructure governed under formal platform structures, operating on a user-pay model with an established fee schedule

description: Clinical environments supporting training and research while delivering healthcare services across multiple disciplines (e.g., physiotherapy, psychology, podiatry). (Université du Québec à Trois-Rivières)

platform: Plateforme de caractérisation chimique et physique de la biomasse
description: Provides analytical tools and expertise for chemical and physical characterization of biomass and bio-based materials.
access: Fee-for-service model with preferential rates for UQTR researchers and affiliated strategic research groups, while external users are charged $60/hour for personnel support and technical services.

description: Advanced laboratory for anatomical training and research using human specimens, supporting education and professional development in health sciences. (Université du Québec à Trois-Rivières)
access: Platform for physical and chemical characterization of biomass and bio-based materials, supporting research, product development, and industrial applications.

description: Supports research on cognition, perception, and brain function using experimental and behavioral neuroscience approaches.
access: No fees or funding model described on that page

description: Research platform supporting studies on neuromusculoskeletal disorders, integrating biomechanics, motor control, and clinical research approaches. (Université du Québec à Trois-Rivières)
access: No fees or funding model described on that page

platform: Services de calcul scientifique et stockage sécurisé
description: Secure research data storage and computing resources for data-intensive projects.
access: Access is granted through a project request and approval process, not through payment.

platform: Chaire de recherche du Canada en environnement et génétique des troubles respiratoires et de l’allergie
domain: Human Genetics / Genomics / Immunology / Respiratory Diseases
description: Research infrastructure focused on understanding the genetic and environmental determinants of asthma and allergic diseases through integrative genomics, microbiome analysis, and multi-omics approaches. The Chair develops predictive, diagnostic, and therapeutic tools and leverages unique family-based cohorts and founder populations to study complex and rare diseases. (UQAC Recherche)

domain: Human Genetics / Population Genetics / Epidemiology / Demography
description: Major research infrastructure consisting of a population database built from Quebec civil records, enabling reconstruction of genealogies and family lineages over nearly four centuries. Supports multidisciplinary research in genetics, epidemiology, demography, and social sciences, including studies of hereditary diseases and founder populations. (UQAC Recherche)
access: Hybrid access model: most historical data is publicly available via CopaQ with a 100-year confidentiality cutoff, while full access to the database is restricted to researchers and requires submission of an access request form.

domain: Public Health / Human Genetics / Epidemiology / Social Sciences
description: Interdisciplinary research centre bringing together researchers from genetics, psychology, social sciences, and informatics to advance sustainable health. The centre develops prevention and intervention models, integrates environmental and genetic determinants of health, and supports data-driven and community-based research addressing major health challenges across the life course. (UQAC Recherche)

unit: Département des sciences de la santé
platform: Laboratoire sur l’adaptation personnelle, sociale et neuropsychologique
description: Research laboratory focused on understanding and improving individual adaptation to life roles, transitions, and stressors. Its research program includes adaptation processes, major life transitions, development and evaluation of interventions, and creation of assessment tools and protocols in psychological and neuropsychological contexts. The lab also serves as a collaborative research environment and training setting for students. (UQAC Recherche)

unit: Département des sciences de la santé
platform: Laboratoire de recherche biomécanique & neurophysiologique en réadaptation neuro-musculo-squelettique (BioNR)
description: Research laboratory focused on neuro-musculoskeletal disorders that lead to physical and functional impairments. The lab develops and applies biomechanical and neurophysiological approaches to study pain, sensorimotor control, non-invasive neurostimulation, aging, and fall prevention. It integrates experimental technologies and clinical research to improve rehabilitation and functional outcomes. (UQAC Recherche)

unit: Institut de biologie intégrative et des systèmes (IBIS)
description: High-throughput sequencing, library preparation, and genomics workflows for basic and translational research.
access: Registration is free, but the platform operates on a fee-for-service model requiring paid submissions for samples or services, with costs handled via standard payment methods and a quote-based billing process before service delivery.

unit: Institut de biologie intégrative et des systèmes (IBIS)
description: Platform providing advanced expertise in optical and electron microscopy as well as histology. Offers support for experimental design, sample preparation, imaging, and data interpretation, along with user training. Equipment includes confocal microscopy, electron microscopy (TEM/SEM), and sample preparation facilities. (Ibis)
access: Pay-per-use fee schedule (per sample, slide, or hourly rental depending on the service), with baseline rates ranging from $4.50 up to $115 per item for Université Laval users, higher pricing for external users (non-profit +15%, business +100%), and discounted rates for rework cases.

unit: Institut de biologie intégrative et des systèmes (IBIS)
domain: Bioinformatics, Genomics, Systems Biology, Data analysis
description: Provides computational biology and bioinformatics support including data analysis, software support, and guidance for omics and sequencing projects
access: Paid institutional HPC service, with access costing $350 per user account per year for compute access, plus $650 per 1 TB of storage, and requires valid institutional accounts and system-specific registrations to use the infrastructure.

platform: VALERIA – Plateforme de gestion et d’exploitation des données de recherche
domain: Data Science / Bioinformatics / Research Data Management
description: Integrated digital research infrastructure designed to facilitate the storage, management, analysis, and sharing of research data. VALERIA provides secure, high-performance computing environments, large-scale storage (S3, Lustre), data collection tools (e.g., REDCap), and collaborative resources to support multidisciplinary research projects. It is accessible to Université Laval researchers and their collaborators, with subsidized core resources and additional fee-based services. (doc.s3.valeria.science)
access: Hybrid funding model with subsidized baseline resources for Université Laval faculty (e.g., free allocations of compute, storage, and 2 hours of support per project) and paid services for additional usage, including storage and support billed at cost plus 15%, with compute resources shared under an institutional “fair use” allocation system rather than direct per-use pricing.

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: Provides NGS (Illumina), Sanger sequencing, gene expression (qPCR/dPCR), RNAi/shRNA, bioinformatics, metabolomics & proteomics services. (crchudequebec.ulaval.ca)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: Advanced protein analysis by mass spectrometry, part of the integrated Genomics Center. (crchudequebec.ulaval.ca)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: High-parameter flow cytometry analysis & cell sorting (Cytek Northern Lights, 30+ markers). (crchudequebec.ulaval.ca)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: Advanced imaging support as part of the Bioimaging & Cytometry platform (fluorescence imaging, high-resolution microscopy). (crchudequebec.ulaval.ca)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
unit: Regenerative Medicine, Tissue Engineering & Biomedical Engineering
description: 3D cell culture, tissue engineering and model development services. (crchudequebec.ulaval.ca)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: Animal housing & models for preclinical research. (crchudequebec.ulaval.ca)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: Offers clinical research support including training, ethics, methodology, biostatistics, and project support. (crchudequebec.ulaval.ca)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: Offers expertise and infrastructure for health and social services data analysis & exploitation. (Innovation News Network)

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
unit: Institut de biologie intégrative et des systèmes (IBIS)
platform: Plateforme de microscopie et imagerie cellulaire
description: Confocal and fluorescence microscopy with technical support and user training.

institution: CHU de Québec-Université Laval Research Centre
description: Study design, statistical analysis, and methodological support for research projects.

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Vector core facility service for gene transfer tech, molecular biology, transgenic animal generation, and viral vectors. Includes design & production of constructs and viral vectors. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: FACS-based cell sorting for separation of neuronal and cell subpopulations; assistance with experimental design and cytometry operations. (neuroquebec.com)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Infrastructure for primary neuronal cultures and related preparation techniques. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Advanced biophotonics facility for brain research diagnostics and phototherapy technologies; clean rooms, microscopy, tissue culture, wet labs for advanced optical methods. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Provides statistical support tailored to neuroscience research (design, analysis, interpretation). (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Advanced transmission electron microscopy capabilities located within the Neurophotonics Centre. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: High-field MRI platform for structural and functional brain imaging (3 T and other imaging modalities). (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Repository of human brain tissue for neuroscience research; important for neurodegenerative and psychiatric disease studies. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Thematic initiative integrating personalized therapy research and individualized diagnostic approaches. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
unit: Centre d’études sur les troubles du sommeil (CETS)
description: Dedicated unit for research on sleep and related disorders within the CERVO ecosystem. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Shared graphics and visualization support for scientific communication and dissemination. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CERVO
description: Audio/video media platform for CERVO research content and outreach. (cervo.ulaval.ca)

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CIRRIS
description: Shared laboratory environment supporting experimental and translational rehabilitation research.

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CIRRIS
description: Provides statistical consulting and analysis support for study design and data interpretation.

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CIRRIS
description: Support for qualitative methodologies including interviews, focus groups, participatory observation, and thematic analysis.

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CIRRIS
unit: Plateforme de collecte et d’analyse du mouvement
description: Instrumentation and tools for capturing and analyzing human movement (e.g., gait, posture, upper/lower limb dynamics).

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CIRRIS
description: Shared research platform focusing on rehabilitation technologies (robots, wearables, assistive devices).

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CIRRIS
platform: Participation sociale et villes inclusives (PSVI Platform)
description: Interdisciplinary platform dedicated to research on social participation and inclusive urban environments for people with disabilities.

institution: Université Laval - Centre de recherche CIRRIS
description: Platform applying virtual environments and digital simulation tools to support rehabilitation research and virtual intervention testing.
Human biospecimen collection and storage to support research (various tissues, cells, samples).
Provides analysis pipelines, data management, and expert computational support.
Controlled biosafety facility for research with infectious agents.
Services include tissue processing, staining, and slide prep.
Offers cell phenotyping and cell sorting analysis using multi-parameter flow cytometry.
Image acquisition & analysis tools, often supporting PET/MRI/optical imaging in research contexts.
Mass-spectrometry driven proteomics and small molecule analyses, including LC-MS workflows.
Preclinical imaging capabilities for rodents (e.g., micro-CT/μPET etc.).
Administrative support for technology platforms, bookings, pricing inquiries.

unit: Centre for Outcomes Research and Evaluation (CORE)
description: Provides comprehensive biostatistics support across the full research lifecycle, including study design, sample size calculations, randomization, data management plans, statistical analysis, and reporting. Supports clinical trials, epidemiological studies, translational research, and large database analyses (e.g., RAMQ, StatCan). Also offers training and knowledge transfer.

unit: Centre for Outcomes Research and Evaluation (CORE)
platform: Capture and Optimization of Research Data (CORD) Unit
domain: Data Management / Clinical Research / Epidemiology / Bioinformatics
description: Provides comprehensive support for research data lifecycle management, including study data planning, database design (REDCap), data collection tools, data cleaning, integration, and version control. Offers methodological guidance, technical support, and infrastructure to ensure high-quality, standardized, and reproducible research data. Works closely with digital infrastructure teams and large-scale research programs (e.g., Maelstrom).

domain: Clinical Data / Epidemiology / Health Informatics / Population Health
description: Centralized data infrastructure integrating clinical and administrative health data from multiple MUHC systems. Enables secure access to large-scale datasets for research, supporting patient cohort selection, variable definition, and advanced health data analysis. Provides consultation services, data extraction (aggregate, de-identified, or identifiable), and tools such as data dictionaries and summaries to facilitate study design and execution. Strong emphasis on data governance, security, and compliance.

domain: Cardiovascular Research, Respiratory Research, Clinical Physiology
description: Dedicated clinical research platform offering cardiopulmonary exercise testing, pulmonary function testing, ECG, vascular assessments, ultrasound, stress echocardiography, exercise testing, sleep studies and related services. (McGill University Health Centre)
access: Fee-for-service; equipment and assisted services available through CIM booking system. (RimuHC)

description: Provides project management, monitoring, data management, REDCap support, biostatistics, regulatory submissions, and multicentre clinical trial support (Phase I-IV and registries). (McGill University Health Centre)
access: Fee-for-service consultation and study management support. (McGill University Health Centre)

description: Clinical imaging support platform within CIM providing imaging services and access to specialized equipment for clinical studies and trials. Mentioned as one of the dedicated clinical research platforms at CIM. (McGill University Health Centre)
access: Fee-for-service; platform access through CIM. (McGill University Health Centre)

description: Research-dedicated Siemens Prisma 3T MRI facility supporting clinical and preclinical imaging, protocol development, data analysis, EEG/TMS integration, physiological monitoring and researcher training. (McGill University Health Centre)
access: Subscription model (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) and pay-per-use access. (McGill University Health Centre)

domain: Health Technology, Medical Devices, Translational Research
description: Hospital-based innovation hub supporting HealthTech development through clinical consultation, mentorship, rapid prototyping, laboratory access, product trials and commercialization support. (McGill University Health Centre)
access: Collaborative access; space and services available to industry and researchers. (McGill University Health Centre)

unit: Centre for Outcomes Research and Evaluation (CORE)
platform: Capture and Optimization of Research Data (CORD) Unit
domain: Data Management, Research Informatics, Clinical Research
description: Provides expertise, software support, methodological guidance, data capture, quality control, integration, cleaning, processing, maintenance and dissemination throughout the study lifecycle. (McGill University Health Centre)
access: Consultation and project-based support. (McGill University Health Centre)

description: Provides animal housing, veterinary services, protocol support, training, regulatory compliance, and technical support for animal-based research across RI-MUHC sites. (RimuHC)
access: Fee-for-service vivarium and animal research support. (RimuHC)

description: Animal housing and phenotyping services to support in vivo research.
Technical facility supporting advanced methodological workflows across research projects.
Biological specimen repository and processing.
High containment facility for select agents.
Flow cytometry and cell sorting services.
High-resolution cellular imaging including confocal & specialized systems.
Functional assays for cell physiology research.
Genetic modification and animal model creation.
Experimental imaging systems for studies.
Immune response profiling and immune assays.
Metabolic profiling and analytics.
Microfluidic device fabrication & workflows.
Histology, IHC, IF, staining, digital pathology.
Drug metabolism and PK studies.
Engages patients and public in research design & dissemination.
Cyclotron facility and radiochemistry support for imaging agents.

platform: CITADEL (Centre for the Integration and Analysis of Medical Data)
domain: Data Science / Clinical Data / Bioinformatics / Epidemiology
description: Data science platform combining a large-scale clinical data lake with advanced analytical expertise. Integrates clinical, administrative, and research data from over 30 information systems to support research projects, including cohort building, data extraction, bioinformatics, biostatistics, and AI-driven analytics. The platform supports innovation in health data science and enables large-scale analyses using millions of patient records and care episodes.

platform: Small Animal Phenotyping and Imaging Platform
domain: Animal Models / In Vivo Imaging / Physiology
description: Provides in vivo phenotyping and imaging services for small animal models, supporting metabolic, physiological, and functional studies with advanced imaging technologies and experimental expertise.

Platform dedicated to the development and production of innovative therapies, including gene and cell therapies. Provides infrastructure and expertise for vector production, translational research, and preclinical development.
Animal care, animal housing & research support for preclinical studies.
Data analysis pipelines, computational support, and integrative omics analyses.
Access to modern gene manipulation and functional genomics methods.
High-parameter flow cytometry for cell phenotyping and quantification.
CRISPR and other genome editing technologies, model system support.
Advanced microscopy (e.g., confocal), imaging workflows, and support.
Mass spectrometry and proteomic profiling support.

unit: Research Platform on Obesity, Metabolism & Diabetes
description: Multidisciplinary platform focused on metabolic research.
Oxford Nanopore long-fragment and multi-omic sequencing capabilities (PromethION, MinION, library prep, consultation). (research.chusj.org)
Flow cytometry, cell phenotype analysis and quantitative cytometry workflows.
Screening support for drug discovery and pharmacology projects.
Wide range of light microscopy systems, analysis tools, training/support for imaging workflows.
Characterization & quantification of small molecules, lipidomics, metabolome profiling with high-resolution mass spectrometry (CFI-funded). (research.chusj.org)
Support for in vivo and preclinical studies and models (e.g., animal or other experimental systems).
Support for proteome profiling and immunopeptidomics research.
CRISPR and cellular reprogramming support (Cas-tech gene editing platform).
contact: Basma Benabdallah (reprogramming) and Romain Gioia (gene editing)

description: Mass spectroscopy assays of drug molecules & metabolites (part of research unit).

platform: Biobanque de la Cohorte hospitalière de l’ICM

Pharmacogenomic analyses and integration of genetic variants with drug responses. (Institut de cardiologie de Montréal)

domain: Cardiovascular Health / Prevention / Exercise Physiology / Clinical Research
description: Multidisciplinary centre dedicated to preventive cardiology and physical activity. Provides clinical evaluation, risk factor management, and lifestyle interventions (exercise, nutrition, stress management), alongside research infrastructure including specialized laboratories and technical platforms. Supports research in cardiovascular prevention, exercise physiology, neuropsychology, and health behavior, integrating clinical care and research across the full prevention continuum. (Institut de cardiologie de Montréal)
Core echocardiography imaging support for research studies. (Institut de cardiologie de Montréal)

access: Fee-for-service; access available to IUCPQ researchers and external academic/industry users.

institution: Institut Universitaire de Cardiologie et de Pneumologie de Quebec (IUCPQ)
platform: Centre for Integration and Governance of Health Information for Secondary Use (CIRRUS)
description: Institutional data access platform that governs, manages, and provides secure access to clinical and administrative health data for secondary use in research, innovation, quality improvement, performance evaluation, teaching, and healthcare management. CIRRUS maintains an institutional data lake, performs data extraction, linkage, de-identification, transformation, and preparation of research-ready datasets, and offers feasibility assessments, participant identification, data management, and methodological support. (iucpq.ca)
access: Access by request through the CIRRUS Portal; some services are fee-for-service under service agreements, while institutional quality/performance requests may not require service agreements. Pricing schedule under development. (iucpq.ca)

domain: Imaging / Flow Cytometry / Proteomics / Cell Biology / Genomics
